ucs.title: [LLM] Deep Panda (Shell Crew) regsvr32 'Squiblydoo' COM scriptlet execution
ucs.description: Hunts the regsvr32 /s /n /u /i:http(s)://... scrobj.dll pattern that Deep Panda / Shell Crew popularised to bypass application whitelisting and deliver in-memory payloads. The combination of regsvr32 with the /i URL switch and scrobj.dll is the actor's signature LOLBin invocation, distinct from generic regsvr32 abuse.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.process_id) as process_id values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" (Processes.process="*scrobj.dll*" OR Processes.process="*/i:http*" OR Processes.process="*/i:\\\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process,"(?i)/i:(https?://|\\\\\\\\)") AND match(process,"(?i)/(s|n|u)\\b") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Deep Panda / Shell Crew Squiblydoo: regsvr32 /s /n /u /i:URL scrobj.dll
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where cmd has "/i:" or cmd has "-i:"
| where cmd matches regex @"(?i)/i:(https?://|\\\\)"
   or cmd has "scrobj.dll"
| where cmd has_any ("/s", "/u", "/n")  // silent + unregister flags Deep Panda used
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Deep Panda / Shell Crew Squiblydoo: regsvr32 /s /n /u /i:URL scrobj.dll
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\regsvr32.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| where cmd matches regex @"(?i)/i:(https?://|\\\\)"
   or cmd has "scrobj.dll"
| where cmd has_any ("/s", "/u", "/n")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Deep Panda Squiblydoo Regsvr32 Remote Scriptlet Execution
id: 8c4d2a1e-9f3b-4f0e-9e87-7b1d2f5e0a11
status: experimental
description: Detects regsvr32.exe executing a remote .sct/COM scriptlet (Squiblydoo), a signature TTP of Deep Panda / Shell Crew.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0009/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1218/010/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.g0009
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
  selection_remote:
    CommandLine|re|i: '/i:(https?://|\\\\)'
  selection_scrobj:
    CommandLine|contains: 'scrobj.dll'
  selection_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/s'
      - '/u'
      - '/n'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_img and (selection_remote or selection_scrobj) and selection_flags and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Rare legitimate enterprise software updaters that use COM scriptlets
level: high
ucs.rationale: Deep Panda / Shell Crew is one of the actors most strongly associated with the regsvr32+scrobj.dll Squiblydoo bypass (originally publicised by Casey Smith and reported on Carbon Black/CrowdStrike Shell Crew intrusions). Tying together regsvr32 + the /i: remote URL switch + the silent/unregister flags + scrobj.dll is the actor's exact invocation, not a generic T1218.010 template — legitimate Window

ucs.title: [LLM] Deep Panda China Chopper IIS web shell -> wmiprvse-spawned WMIC lateral discovery
ucs.description: Catches Deep Panda's hallmark post-exploitation chain: a China Chopper-style web shell in an IIS w3wp.exe worker process spawning cmd/PowerShell/wmic/net.exe for host & remote-system discovery and SMB lateral movement (T1505.003 -> T1057/T1018 -> T1047/T1021.002). Anchored on w3wp.exe parents because the Anthem and other Shell Crew intrusions used .aspx web shells dropped under inetpub as the foothold.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_processes dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_children from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","hostname.exe","qwinsta.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id _time span=10m | where distinct_children >= 2 OR match(cmdlines,"(?i)(wmic\s+/node|net\s+(use|view|group\s+\"Domain\s+Admins\")|nltest\s+/dclist|tasklist\s+/svc|systeminfo)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Deep Panda / Shell Crew: China Chopper-style w3wp.exe web shell spawning recon/lateral tooling
let recon_bins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","net.exe",
                          "net1.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe",
                          "tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe",
                          "qwinsta.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe"]);
let recon_keywords = dynamic(["/node:","net use","net view","net group","domain admins",
                              "nltest /dclist","tasklist /svc","systeminfo","qwinsta",
                              "\\\\admin$","\\\\c$","\\\\ipc$","process call create"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe")
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wmiprvse.exe" and InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "w3wp.exe")
| where FileName in~ (recon_bins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| summarize EventCount = count(),
            FirstSeen   = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen    = max(Timestamp),
            ChildBins   = make_set(FileName, 25),
            DistinctChildren = dcount(FileName),
            CmdLines    = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
            ReconHits   = countif(cmd has_any (recon_keywords))
            by DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessId
| where DistinctChildren >= 2 or ReconHits >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Deep Panda / Shell Crew: China Chopper-style IIS w3wp.exe spawning recon / lateral tools
let recon_bins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","net.exe",
                          "net1.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe",
                          "tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe",
                          "qwinsta.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe"]);
let recon_keywords = dynamic(["/node:","net use","net view","net group","domain admins",
                              "nltest /dclist","tasklist /svc","systeminfo","qwinsta",
                              "\\\\admin$","\\\\c$","\\\\ipc$","process call create"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\wmiprvse.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(ChildBin) in (recon_bins)
| extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| summarize EventCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            ChildBins = make_set(ChildBin, 25),
            DistinctChildren = dcount(ChildBin),
            CmdLines  = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            ReconHits = countif(cmd has_any (recon_keywords))
            by Computer, Account, ParentProcessName
| where DistinctChildren >= 2 or ReconHits >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Deep Panda (a.k.a. Shell Crew, WebMasters, Black Vine) earned the 'Shell Crew' moniker because their primary foothold tradecraft on IIS-hosted infrastructure is dropping ASPX/JSP web shells (China Chopper, Derusbi loaders) and then pivoting via WMI/WMIC and SMB admin shares — exactly the chain seen in the Anthem breach (RSA Shell Crew, CrowdStrike, Symantec Black Vine reporting). w3wp.exe spawning
